courses.programme: Degree Programme in Computer Science and Engineering
courses.title: Introduction to Programming
courses.name: Introduction to Programming
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Write, compile and run simple programs - Understand basic control structures (loops, conditionals) - Apply fundamental data structures (arrays, lists)
courses.content: This course covers the basics of programming using Python: variables, control flow, functions, basic data structures, and simple algorithms. Includes weekly exercises and a final project.

courses.programme: Degree Programme in Computer Science and Engineering
courses.title: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Analyze algorithm complexity using Big-O notation - Implement common data structures (trees, graphs, hash tables) - Select appropriate algorithms for a given problem
courses.content: Covers trees, graphs, hash tables, sorting and searching algorithms, and algorithm analysis. Includes lab sessions and programming assignments.

courses.title: Protein Chemistry I
courses.name: Protein Chemistry I
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Tell the relevance of proteins, their structures and function - Understand methods for purifying recombinant proteins - Analyze biochemical data related to protein/enzyme purification
courses.content: This module provides detailed information on the chemistry of proteins, including protein folding, degradation, enzyme catalysis, and purification/characterization methods. Includes lectures and laboratory exercises.
courses.prerequisites: Biomolecules for Biochemists, Biochemical methodologies I

courses.programme: Courses for exchange students - Centre for Language and Communication Studies
courses.title: Academic English Writing
courses.name: Academic English Writing
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Structure academic texts clearly - Use appropriate academic register and citation practices - Give and receive peer feedback on written drafts
courses.content: Focuses on academic writing conventions, structuring arguments, citation practices, and peer review. Suitable for exchange students at any level.
